name: Mesa River Gorges Loop
name_original: Río Mesa y Santuario de Jaraba — circular desde Mochales
description: This 37.8-mile moderate road cycling route from Mochales explores the Mesa River canyon, featuring the Pozo Redondo Waterfall and Santuario
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Mochales navigate a landscape characterized by river gorges, sierras, and forests. The terrain features varied elevations, including moderate climbs and more challenging ascents through natural areas. Road cyclists can expect routes that traverse open countryside and pass by historical points of interest. Best road cycling routes around Mochales The most popular road cycling route is Mesa River Gorges Loop, a 37.8 miles (60.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 59 minutes to complete, offering views of the Mesa River gorges. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Sierra de Solorio and Castillo de los Funes Loop, a moderate 30.7 miles (49.4 km) path, winding through the Sierra de Solorio and past historical sites. Local road cyclists also love the Gorges of the Jalón River – Medinaceli loop from Iruecha, a 59.0 miles (94.9 km) trail leading through the dramatic Gorges of the Jalón River, often completed in about 4 hours 24 minutes. Road cycling around Mochales is defined by river gorges, sierras, and forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging climbs. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 40 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Mochales's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Mochales?
A: There are 7 road cycling routes documented in the Mochales area, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Mochales?
A: Road cycling routes around Mochales navigate a landscape characterized by river gorges, sierras, and forests. The terrain features varied elevations, including moderate climbs and more challenging ascents through natural areas. You can expect routes that traverse open countryside and pass by historical points of interest.

Q: Are there options for less experienced road cyclists in Mochales?
A: While there are no routes specifically marked as 'easy', several moderate routes are available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018205208/mesa-river-gorges-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mesa River Gorges Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option, covering 37.8 miles (60.8 km) with a duration of just under 3 hours, offering a good balance of challenge and scenic views.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced road cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143120" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorges of the Jalón River – Medinaceli loop from Iruecha&lt;/a&gt; offer a difficult 59.0 miles (94.9 km) ride with significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31460758" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maranchón Juniper Forest – Maranchón loop from Codes&lt;/a&gt;, which is 83.9 miles (135.0 km) long and features over 1100 meters of climbing.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Mochales?
A: Many of the road cycling routes in Mochales are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018205208/mesa-river-gorges-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mesa River Gorges Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018201461/sierra-de-solorio-and-castillo-de-los-funes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Solorio and Castillo de los Funes Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical sites can I see while road cycling in Mochales?
A: Several routes pass by historical points of interest. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018201461/sierra-de-solorio-and-castillo-de-los-funes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Solorio and Castillo de los Funes Loop&lt;/a&gt; winds past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5414352" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Funes&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter charming settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5379689" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villel de Mesa&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2682907" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iruecha&lt;/a&gt;, and religious buildings such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5306748" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Concha&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any waterfalls along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region around Mochales is known for its waterfalls. While specific routes may not pass directly by all of them, you can find natural attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2483262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Algar de Mesa Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6909091" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mesa River Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7078639" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo Galano Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; in the vicinity, which can be explored as detours or part of a longer ride.

Q: What natural attractions can I explore on a road bike in Mochales?
A: Beyond the river gorges and sierras, you can explore areas like the Maranchón Juniper Forest, which is featured in the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31460758" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maranchón Juniper Forest – Maranchón loop from Codes&lt;/a&gt;. The dramatic Gorges of the Jalón River are also a significant natural highlight, accessible via the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143120" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorges of the Jalón River – Medinaceli loop from Iruecha&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find long-distance road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, Mochales offers several long-distance road cycling routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143120" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorges of the Jalón River – Medinaceli loop from Iruecha&lt;/a&gt; is nearly 60 miles (94.9 km) long, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38155965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcolea del Pinar – Anguita Village and Church loop from Codes&lt;/a&gt; stretches over 90 miles (145 km), providing ample distance for endurance riders.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Mochales?
A: The komoot community frequently praises the varied terrain, including river gorges, sierras, and forests, which define the road cycling experience in Mochales. Riders appreciate the options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging climbs, and the opportunity to pass by historical points of interest.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through villages or towns where I might find cafes?
A: While specific cafe locations are not detailed in the route descriptions, many routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38155965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcolea del Pinar – Anguita Village and Church loop from Codes&lt;/a&gt;, pass through villages like Anguita and Alcolea del Pinar. These settlements are likely places where you could find local amenities, including cafes, for a break.
tips: We are next to the castle of Calmarza, where we can see a stately tower whose origin is unknown. It is 12 meters tall.
An essential corner for nature lovers. The "Pozo Redondo" Waterfall of Calmarza is a charming place that is hidden in the lower part of the town where the Mesa River passes

At this point we can take the detour to approach the Sanctuary of Jaraba, an imposing and impressive hermitage located in the Barranco de la Hoz Seca, with rocky walls next to the river canyon. A highly recommended visit if we are in the area. The guided tour is recommended.
Hermitage which is a small Catholic temple located near Hinojosa, rural Romanesque style built in the second half of the twelfth century. It is of small dimensions.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Rio Mesa y Santuario de Jaraba cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 60.8 kilometers long and is generally considered a moderate ride. While individual times vary based on fitness and stops, komoot estimates an average duration of around 3 hours for this racebike tour.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While primarily a road cycling route, the trail research indicates there may be unpaved or difficult sections for a road bike. You'll be cycling through impressive canyons carved into limestone and tufa rock formations along the Mesa River, so be prepared for varied surfaces and potentially challenging stretches.

faqs.question: Where does the route start and where can I park in Mochales?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular tour starting and ending in Mochales. While specific parking details aren't provided, Mochales serves as the primary access point for this loop. Look for suitable parking areas within the village.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Rio Mesa y Santuario de Jaraba route?
faqs.answer: This route offers stunning natural beauty and historical sites. Key highlights include the Pozo Redondo Waterfall in Calmarza, the dramatic canyon views along the Mesa River, and the Santuario de Jaraba. You can also spot the Calmarza Tower and the Santa Catalina Hermitage.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for all types of road bikes, given the mention of difficult sections?
faqs.answer: Due to the possibility of unpaved or difficult sections, as noted in the trail research, riders should exercise caution. While it's primarily a racebike route, some parts might be challenging for a pure road bike and could require careful navigation or even dismounting. Consider your bike's capabilities and your comfort level with varied terrain.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The trail follows the Mesa River through canyons, which can be particularly beautiful in spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be hot, while winter might bring colder conditions. Always check local weather forecasts before you go.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is primarily a road cycling tour. While there are no specific restrictions mentioned regarding dogs on the road sections, it's always advisable to keep dogs leashed and under control, especially in areas with traffic or near natural attractions. Be mindful of local regulations and other cyclists.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other trails. You will find yourself on parts of the Jaraba-Calmarza trail, Ibdes-Jaraba, Senda a la Cueva y Fuente del Moro, and Llumes - Calmarza, among others. These intersections offer glimpses into the broader network of paths in the region.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route or access the surrounding natural park?
faqs.answer: The route passes through the area of Parameras de Maranchón, Hoz del Mesa y Aragoncillo. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public roads or trails in this region. However, always respect local regulations and private property, especially if you venture off the main route.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near the route, particularly in Mochales or Jaraba?
faqs.answer: Both Mochales and Jaraba are villages that may offer basic amenities. Jaraba is known for its thermal spas and likely has more options for cafes, restaurants, and accommodation. It's a good idea to check in advance for opening hours, especially in smaller villages.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the Mesa River valley?
faqs.answer: You can expect overwhelmingly beautiful canyon views along the Mesa River. The scenery is characterized by narrow walls formed by unique geological features, including impressive limestone and tufa rock formations. It's a truly immersive experience within a naturally stunning valley.
